Jorge Gomez, EIT

Electrical Engineering Student & Engineering Intern

Electrical Engineering student at the University of California, Riverside with hands-on experience in power distribution, ArcMaps, EV infrastructure, solar microgrids, BESS, project management and embedded systems. Engineer in Training certified. Building real infrastructure for Moreno Valley, CA.

Engineering Projects
Utility Study
CPAT Load Flow Analysis

Conducted load flow case studies to optimize reactive power and voltage profiles across a municipal distribution simulation. Tuned generator setpoints, capacitor banks, synchronous condensers, and solar resources.

CPATLoad Flow Analysis

Electrical Design
Solar Microgrid Design

Designed full electrical plans for a municipal solar microgrid: single-line diagrams, inverter and transformer selection, switchgear layout, conductor sizing, grounding, and Title 24-compliant lighting.

Electrical Design
EV Charging Infrastructure

Developed electrical infrastructure plans for 10 Level 3 and 10 Level 2 EV chargers. Performed load calculations, transformer and distribution panel configuration, and ensured ADA/CBC compliance.

Embedded Systems
Autonomous RC Car

Integrated a Slamtec LiDAR with Python on a Raspberry Pi to map 2D environments and perform obstacle-avoidance using IMU data and a PID controller. Implemented autonomous reverse-path retracing.

Automation
Automated Billing System

Built a Python script that extracted customer data from XML and generated formatted PDF bills, producing 1,200+ invoices in under 5 minutes — eliminating manual billing workflows for city operations.
